response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of solid software engineering experience, but the role requires a dedicated C++ developer with industrial-automation and IEC 62443 secure-engineering background. C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis; C++/STL is not in the listed skills. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or SDL/IEC 62443 work. The candidate's strengths lie in web/full-stack (JS/TS, Node, DLang), which only partially overlap with the 'advantageous' stack.
strengths: 15+ years of senior engineering across web, mobile, embedded and distributed systems
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, mentoring, public workshops
Some low-latency, multi-service, networked-systems experience (Sociomantic, GISCollective)
concerns: Minimal hands-on C++ experience and no C++/STL in current skill set
No industrial-automation, IEC 62443, or SDL secure-engineering background
No evidence of MQTT, message brokers, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)

key_factors.factor: Senior engineering maturity and mentoring track record
